SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.store.jdbc.serializer.cipher

Class AesGcmPayloadCipher

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.store.jdbc.serializer.cipher.AesGcmPayloadCipher
All Implemented Interfaces:
PayloadCipher
public class AesGcmPayloadCipher extends Object implements PayloadCipher
Production-grade PayloadCipher using AES-GCM (authenticated encryption) — the built-in option for real payload-at-rest confidentiality in the JDBC store (audit A4).

Format

Each encrypt(java.lang.String) call generates a fresh random 16-byte IV (nonce) and produces Base64( IV (16 bytes) || ciphertext+GCM-tag ). A random IV per write means encrypting the same plaintext twice yields different ciphertext (semantic security) and is mandatory for GCM — never reuse an IV with the same key. The 128-bit GCM tag authenticates the ciphertext, so decrypt(java.lang.String) throws (rather than returning garbage) if a stored row was tampered with or was encrypted under a different key.

Key

The key is supplied at construction as raw bytes and must be a valid AES key length: 16, 24, or 32 bytes (AES-128 / 192 / 256). Provide a high-entropy key from a secret manager / KMS / env var — never a hard-coded or password-derived constant. See fromBase64(String) for the common Base64-encoded-key path used by the auto-configuration.

Rotation

The EncryptingSerializer dispatches reads by the ENC(<id>:...) tag, so rows stay readable across cipher changes as long as the cipher that wrote them is still registered. To rotate the AES key, keep the old key's cipher available for reads while writing with the new one (e.g. via distinct registered ids).

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:

    Decrypts raw ciphertext (no envelope) back to the serialized payload string.

    Encrypts a serialized payload string to raw ciphertext (no envelope).

  • Constructor Details

    • AesGcmPayloadCipher

      public AesGcmPayloadCipher(byte[] keyBytes)
      Creates an AES-GCM cipher from raw key bytes.
      Parameters:
      keyBytes - the AES key; must be 16, 24, or 32 bytes long (AES-128/192/256)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the key length is not a valid AES key size

  • Method Details

    • fromBase64

      public static AesGcmPayloadCipher fromBase64(String base64Key)
      Creates an AES-GCM cipher from a Base64-encoded key — the form bound from failover.store.jdbc.encryption.key.
      Parameters:
      base64Key - the Base64-encoded AES key (decodes to 16, 24, or 32 bytes)
      Returns:
      a cipher using the decoded key
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is not valid Base64 or decodes to an invalid key length

    • id

      public String id()
      Description copied from interface: PayloadCipher
      Short, stable, unique identifier persisted in the ENC(<id>:...) envelope and used on read to select this cipher. Example: "b64", "aesgcm".
      Specified by:
      id in interface PayloadCipher
      Returns:
      the cipher id; must not be null, empty, or contain ':' or ')'

    • encrypt

      public @Nullable String encrypt(@Nullable String plaintext)
      Description copied from interface: PayloadCipher
      Encrypts a serialized payload string to raw ciphertext (no envelope).
      Specified by:
      encrypt in interface PayloadCipher
      Parameters:
      plaintext - the serialized payload; null is allowed
      Returns:
      the raw ciphertext, or null if plaintext is null

    • decrypt

      public @Nullable String decrypt(@Nullable String ciphertext)
      Description copied from interface: PayloadCipher
      Decrypts raw ciphertext (no envelope) back to the serialized payload string.
      Specified by:
      decrypt in interface PayloadCipher
      Parameters:
      ciphertext - the raw ciphertext previously returned by PayloadCipher.encrypt(java.lang.String); null is allowed
      Returns:
      the serialized payload, or null if ciphertext is null
